title: Cannot perform  "podman build" within sshfs-mounted directory
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Unable to perform **$> podman build** within _sshfs-mounted_ directory.

The error seems _similar_ to https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/2774
However, that bug report describes building an image within a container. This bug report describes building an image directly on the container host.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
```
otheruser@otherhost $> tree ~/images/myimage
    myimage
    ├── Dockerfile
    ├── docker-php-ext-configure
otheruser@otherhost $> cat ~/images/myimage/Dockerfile
    FROM debian:buster-slim
    COPY docker-php-ext-*  /usr/local/bin/

regularuser@containerhost $> sshfs otheruser@otherhost:images/ ~/images
regularuser@containerhost $> cd ~/images/myimage
regularuser@containerhost $> podman build .
```

**Describe the results you received:**
Failure building otherwise valid image
```
STEP 2: COPY docker-php-ext-*  /usr/local/bin/
copier: get: "/docker-php-ext-configure": error getting extended attributes for "/docker-php-ext-configure": error listing extended attributes of "/docker-php-ext-configure": operation not supported
```
**Describe the results you expected:**
Successful image build

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Happens consistently and reproducably.

If the sshfs-mounted directory is _copied_ to containerhost, the build occurs without error
The copy-the-dir approach is a work-around, but not a good solution for me
```
    $> cp -a ~/images ~/images-local
    $> cd ~/images-local/myimage
    $> podman build .
```

**Output of `podman version`:**
```
        Version:      2.1.1
        API Version:  2.0.0
        Go Version:   go1.14
        Built:        Wed Dec 31 16:00:00 1969
        OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**
```
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.16.1
  cgroupManager: cgroupfs
  cgroupVersion: v1
  conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon'
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.20, commit: '
  cpus: 4
  distribution:
    distribution: debian
    version: "10"
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: arachne
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 2000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100001
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 2000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100001
      size: 65536
  kernel: 4.19.0-10-amd64
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 369057792
  memTotal: 2091732992
  ociRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: 'runc: /usr/sbin/runc'
    path: /usr/sbin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0~rc6+dfsg1
      commit: 1.0.0~rc6+dfsg1-3
      spec: 1.0.1
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/2000/podman/podman.sock
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: 'slirp4netns: /usr/bin/slirp4netns'
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.1.4
      commit: unknown
      libslirp: 4.3.1-git
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
  swapFree: 746057728
  swapTotal: 1073737728
  uptime: 184h 14m 26.29s (Approximately 7.67 days)
registries:
  search:
  - docker.io
  - quay.io
version:
  APIVersion: 2.0.0
  Built: 0
  BuiltTime: Wed Dec 31 16:00:00 1969
  GitCommit: ""
  GoVersion: go1.14
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 2.1.1
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**
```
podman/unknown,now 2.1.1~2 amd64 [installed]
podman/unknown 2.1.1~2 arm64
podman/unknown 2.1.1~2 armhf
podman/unknown 2.1.1~2 ppc64el
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Container host is a VirtualBox VM running Debian Buster
VirtualBox VM host is Fedora 32
podman packages installed from OpenSuse repo
**$> cat "/etc/apt/sources.list.d/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.list"**
```
## "Kubic" repo from "OpenSuse" for "podman" packages since they aren't in Debian 10 (buster) repos
deb https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/Debian_10/ /
```
**$> cat /etc/os-release** 
`PRETTY_NAME="Debian GNU/Linux 10 (buster)"`

comments.body: @giuseppe I'm guessing this is a fuse-overlay issue?
I believe @nalind might have just made a change to buildah to ignore certain errors in COPY/ADD when Xattrs are not supported.  This might already be fixed in master branch.
Looks like we'll pick it up when we vendor Buildah v1.18

Just tried building a container from a 9p filesystem with buildah 1.18.0 and I'm still getting this error:

```
error getting extended attributes for "/somefile": error listing extended attributes of "/somefile": timer expired
```

> Just tried building a container from a 9p filesystem with buildah 1.18.0 and I'm still getting this error:
> 
> ```
> error getting extended attributes for "/somefile": error listing extended attributes of "/somefile": timer expired
> ```
I test this, work correct, how reproduce your error
```
$ mkdir /root/sshfs
$ sshfs 10.xxx.45.86:/root/test /root/sshfs/
The authenticity of host '10.xxx.45.86 (10.xxx.45.86)' can't be established.
ECDSA key fingerprint is SHA256:d6zNoaohkZG/QFu/bcJ+nCPxxxxxxxxeiHASo.
Are you sure you want to continue connecting (yes/no/[fingerprint])? yes
root@10.xxx.45.86's password: 
$ ll /root/sshfs/
total 4
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 48 Dec 14 11:40 Dockerfile
$ cd /root/sshfs/
$ ll
total 4
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 48 Dec 14 11:40 Dockerfile
$ cat Dockerfile 
FROM docker.io/library/alpine
COPY Dockerf* /
$ podman version
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.13.15
Built:        Wed Dec  9 05:10:52 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
$ podman build .
STEP 1: FROM docker.io/library/alpine
STEP 2: COPY Dockerf* /
--> Using cache 6c2e2856577587edbfa36be039fde5e73d60c324dcc36d431abcc8e841d383d3
--> 6c2e2856577
6c2e2856577587edbfa36be039fde5e73d60c324dcc36d431abcc8e841d383d3
```

```
$ stat /root/sshfs/
  File: /root/sshfs/
  Size: 37        	Blocks: 8          IO Block: 4096   directory
Device: 32h/50d	Inode: 1           Links: 1
Access: (0755/drwxr-xr-x)  Uid: (    0/    root)   Gid: (    0/    root)
Access: 2020-12-14 11:43:54.000000000 +0800
Modify: 2020-12-14 11:43:51.000000000 +0800
Change: 2020-12-14 11:43:51.000000000 +0800
 Birth: -
```
Could be an issue with the 9p server then. In any case, I think Buildah should be less strict when it comes to these errors. Building with `docker` works just fine.

Well podman build including buildah 1.18.0 so I am closing this issue, @monken your issue looks different but I believe it is involved in the underlying file system and not something podman or buildah can deal with.  Maybe fuse-overlay is having an issue.  @giuseppe WDYT?
